title: Kangaroo Level 5-6 - Practice Test 3
disclaimer: Original practice problems inspired by Math Kangaroo format. Not official AKSF content.

questions.question_text: What is the value of 2026 - 1999?
A right angle is split into two smaller angles by a ray. One of them measures 35 degrees. What is the measure of the other?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' font-family='sans-serif'><path d='M 30 120 L 170 120 L 30 20 Z' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='30' y1='120' x2='130' y2='40' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.5'/><text x='45' y='110' font-size='12'>35</text><text x='55' y='80' font-size='12'>?</text></svg>
questions.image_description: A right angle split into a 35-degree angle and an unknown angle.
questions.solution: A right angle measures 90 degrees, so 90 - 35 = 55.

questions.question_text: Which of the following numbers is prime?
Anna, Ben, Carla, Dan, and Eli stand in a line. Anna is first, Eli is last. Ben stands directly before Dan, and Carla stands directly after Anna. In what position is Dan?
questions.solution: Order: Anna, Carla, Ben, Dan, Eli. Dan is 4th.

questions.question_text: A square has side length 6. What is its perimeter?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' font-family='sans-serif'><rect x='20' y='20' width='80' height='80' fill='#e8e8e8' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><text x='55' y='15' font-size='12'>6</text></svg>
questions.image_description: A square labeled with side length 6.

questions.question_text: A pencil costs 40 cents and an eraser costs 25 cents. How much do 2 pencils and 2 erasers cost together?
What number comes next in the pattern 2, 5, 8, 11, 14, ...?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 280 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' font-family='sans-serif'><rect x='10' y='25' width='40' height='30' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.5'/><rect x='55' y='25' width='40' height='30' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.5'/><rect x='100' y='25' width='40' height='30' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.5'/><rect x='145' y='25' width='40' height='30' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.5'/><rect x='190' y='25' width='40' height='30' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.5'/><rect x='235' y='25' width='40' height='30' fill='#e8e8e8' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.5'/><text x='22' y='45' font-size='12'>2</text><text x='67' y='45' font-size='12'>5</text><text x='112' y='45' font-size='12'>8</text><text x='153' y='45' font-size='12'>11</text><text x='198' y='45' font-size='12'>14</text><text x='250' y='45' font-size='12'>?</text></svg>
questions.image_description: A row of boxes with numbers 2, 5, 8, 11, 14, and a final shaded box with a question mark.

questions.question_text: How many unit squares of side 1 fit inside the rectangle shown?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' font-family='sans-serif'><rect x='20' y='20' width='160' height='80' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><text x='90' y='15' font-size='12'>8</text><text x='5' y='65' font-size='12'>4</text></svg>
questions.image_description: A rectangle with width 8 and height 4.

questions.question_text: What is half of 242?
What is the greatest common divisor (GCD) of 36 and 48?
Two straight lines cross. One of the four angles formed measures 40 degrees. What is the measure of the angle vertically opposite to it?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' font-family='sans-serif'><line x1='20' y1='30' x2='180' y2='120' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='20' y1='120' x2='180' y2='30' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><text x='95' y='50' font-size='12'>40</text><text x='95' y='110' font-size='12'>x</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Two crossing lines form vertical angles; one marked 40, the opposite marked x.
questions.solution: Vertical angles are equal, so x = 40.

questions.question_text: A box holds red, blue, and green balls. There are twice as many red as blue, and 3 more green than red. If there are 5 blue balls, how many balls are there in total?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' font-family='sans-serif'><rect x='10' y='30' width='220' height='70' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2' rx='10'/><circle cx='40' cy='65' r='10' fill='#e8e8e8' stroke='currentColor'/><circle cx='70' cy='65' r='10' fill='#e8e8e8' stroke='currentColor'/><circle cx='110' cy='65' r='10' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><circle cx='140' cy='65' r='10' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><circle cx='180' cy='65' r='10' fill='#e8e8e8' stroke='currentColor'/><text x='90' y='20' font-size='11'>R, B, G balls</text></svg>
questions.image_description: A box with mixed red, blue, and green balls inside.

questions.question_text: What is 7 x 8 + 6 x 9?
Point P is at (2, 3) and point Q is at (7, 3) in the coordinate plane. What is the length of segment PQ?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' font-family='sans-serif'><line x1='20' y1='130' x2='180' y2='130' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='20' y1='20' x2='20' y2='130' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1'/><circle cx='50' cy='80' r='3' fill='currentColor'/><circle cx='130' cy='80' r='3' fill='currentColor'/><text x='42' y='75' font-size='11'>P</text><text x='125' y='75' font-size='11'>Q</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Points P(2,3) and Q(7,3) on a coordinate plane.

questions.question_text: A three-digit number has digits that add up to 9. The first digit is 4 and the last digit is 3. What is the middle digit?
How many prime numbers are there strictly between 10 and 25?
A regular hexagon is divided into 6 congruent triangles by lines from its center to its vertices. If the hexagon has area 60, what is the area of one triangle?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 200' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' font-family='sans-serif'><polygon points='100,20 170,60 170,140 100,180 30,140 30,60' fill='#e8e8e8' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='100' y1='100' x2='100' y2='20' stroke='currentColor'/><line x1='100' y1='100' x2='170' y2='60' stroke='currentColor'/><line x1='100' y1='100' x2='170' y2='140' stroke='currentColor'/><line x1='100' y1='100' x2='100' y2='180' stroke='currentColor'/><line x1='100' y1='100' x2='30' y2='140' stroke='currentColor'/><line x1='100' y1='100' x2='30' y2='60' stroke='currentColor'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A regular hexagon split into six congruent triangles from the center.

questions.question_text: A train leaves at 8:45 AM and arrives at 11:20 AM. How long is the trip?
A number is called lucky if it is divisible by 3 but not by 9. How many lucky numbers are there between 1 and 30 inclusive?
questions.solution: Multiples of 3 in 1..30: 10. Multiples of 9: 9, 18, 27 (3 numbers). 10 - 3 = 8.

questions.question_text: A cube is formed by folding the net shown. Square S is the center of the cross. Which labeled square ends up opposite S when the net is folded?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 180' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' font-family='sans-serif'><rect x='40' y='60' width='40' height='40' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.5'/><rect x='80' y='60' width='40' height='40' fill='#e8e8e8' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.5'/><rect x='120' y='60' width='40' height='40' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.5'/><rect x='160' y='60' width='40' height='40' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.5'/><rect x='80' y='20' width='40' height='40' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.5'/><rect x='80' y='100' width='40' height='40' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.5'/><text x='55' y='85' font-size='11'>P</text><text x='95' y='85' font-size='11'>S</text><text x='135' y='85' font-size='11'>Q</text><text x='175' y='85' font-size='11'>R</text><text x='95' y='45' font-size='11'>T</text><text x='95' y='125' font-size='11'>U</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Cross-shaped cube net with center S and squares P (left of S), Q (right of S), R (far right), T (above S), U (below S).
questions.solution: When folded, S is the bottom; P, Q, T, U wrap to the sides; R (two steps from S along the row of four) lands on top, opposite S.

questions.question_text: What is the least common multiple (LCM) of 12 and 18?
A triangle has angles in the ratio 2 : 3 : 4. What is the measure (in degrees) of the largest angle?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' font-family='sans-serif'><polygon points='20,130 200,130 140,20' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><text x='30' y='125' font-size='11'>2</text><text x='180' y='125' font-size='11'>3</text><text x='135' y='40' font-size='11'>4</text></svg>

questions.question_text: Five cards show the digits 1, 2, 3, 4, 5. Three different cards are chosen and arranged to form a three-digit number. How many different three-digit numbers can be formed?

A tank holds 240 liters. An inlet pipe alone fills the tank in 6 hours, and a drain alone empties a full tank in 8 hours. If the tank is empty and both are open, how long does it take to fill the tank?
questions.solution: Inlet 40 L/h, drain 30 L/h, net 10 L/h. 240 / 10 = 24 hours.

questions.question_text: What is the sum 1 + 2 + 3 + ... + 20?
A square with side 10 has a smaller square with side 4 cut out of one corner. What is the area of the remaining L-shape?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 160 160' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' font-family='sans-serif'><path d='M 20 60 L 60 60 L 60 20 L 140 20 L 140 140 L 20 140 Z' fill='#e8e8e8' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><text x='75' y='15' font-size='11'>10</text><text x='30' y='55' font-size='11'>4</text></svg>
questions.image_description: 10x10 square with a 4x4 square removed from the top-left corner forming an L-shape.

questions.question_text: Three children share 24 candies. Each gets at least one, and no two get the same number. What is the greatest possible number of candies one child could receive?
questions.solution: The two smallest distinct positive integers are 1 and 2, so the largest = 24 - 1 - 2 = 21.

questions.question_text: If A = 2^5 and B = 5^2, what is A - B?
A book has pages numbered 1 through 99. How many times does the digit 7 appear in all page numbers combined?
questions.solution: Units digit 7: pages 7, 17, 27, ..., 97 = 10 times. Tens digit 7: pages 70 through 79 = 10 times. Total = 20.
